solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int maxAbsValExpr(int[] arr1, int[] arr2) {
        int max1 = Integer.MIN_VALUE, min1 = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
        int max2 = Integer.MIN_VALUE, min2 = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
        int max3 = Integer.MIN_VALUE, min3 = Integer.MAX_VALUE;
        int max4 = Integer.MIN_VALUE, min4 = Integer.MAX_VALUE;

        for (int i = 0; i < arr1.length; i++) {
            int a = arr1[i] + arr2[i];
            int b = arr1[i] - arr2[i];
            int c = -arr1[i] + arr2[i];
            int d = -arr1[i] - arr2[i];

            max1 = Math.max(max1, a);
            min1 = Math.min(min1, a);
            max2 = Math.max(max2, b);
            min2 = Math.min(min2, b);
            max3 = Math.max(max3, c);
            min3 = Math.min(min3, c);
            max4 = Math.max(max4, d);
            min4 = Math.min(min4, d);
        }

        int res = 0;
        res = Math.max(res, max1 - min1);
        res = Math.max(res, max2 - min2);
        res = Math.max(res, max3 - min3);
        res = Math.max(res, max4 - min4);

        return res;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution leverages the properties of absolute values and rewrites the expression into four different forms that can be maximized independently. It then computes the maximum and minimum values for each form across the array and calculates the maximum difference for each form. The final result is the maximum of these four differences. This approach is efficient and avoids the need for nested loops.
